current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: mixture_topk was slightly worse than the topk anchor under normal runtime, so the next move is to try a different expressive family rather than spending another round on that branch. residual_topk is the most directly FVU-oriented next architecture because it explicitly allocates encoder capacity to residual errors.

Both mixture_topk and residual_topk archived under normal runtime with proxy FVU near the topk proxy anchor, so the next move is another architecture family rather than smaller-K search. factorized_topk is the clearest remaining low-friction expressivity upgrade in the current codebase.

The first factorized_topk prototype was much worse than the anchor, but its implementation used a plain hidden layer with width equal to d_in, which is a likely bottleneck rather than a decisive family verdict. This round keeps the same family and K while upgrading the encoder to a wider gated mixer.

action.hypothesis: A residual two-branch encoder may reduce FVU at K=128 by letting the second encoder pass target what the first branch misses, which is directly aligned with the reconstruction objective. Test residual_topk at the same K=128 anchor point as a quality-first architecture probe.
action.summary: mixture_topk was slightly worse than the topk anchor under normal runtime, so the next move is to try a different expressive family rather than spending another round on that branch. residual_topk is the most directly FVU-oriented next architecture because it explicitly allocates encoder capacity to residual errors.
action.self_review: This keeps the comparison clean and opens a new family with a stronger direct link to reconstruction quality than mixture routing. It is still a first prototype pass, so a mediocre result should not automatically rule out all multi-branch encoders.
notes_to_memory: mixture_topk at K=128/ef8/signum archived under normal runtime with proxy FVU 0.3058621361851692, so soft expert mixing is not an immediate win over the anchor.
Round 3 opens residual_topk as a new quality-first family because its second encoder pass is explicitly aimed at reducing remaining reconstruction error.
next_hypotheses: If residual_topk shows proxy improvement, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before promotion.
If residual_topk is also flat or worse, try factorized_topk next rather than returning to smaller-K search.

action.hypothesis: A factorized nonlinear encoder may lower FVU at K=128 by adding a hidden mixing stage before latent scoring, giving the encoder more expressive feature composition while keeping the fixed-K decode path unchanged. Test factorized_topk at the same K=128 anchor point as the next quality-first family.
action.summary: Both mixture_topk and residual_topk archived under normal runtime with proxy FVU near the topk proxy anchor, so the next move is another architecture family rather than smaller-K search. factorized_topk is the clearest remaining low-friction expressivity upgrade in the current codebase.
action.self_review: This is still exploratory, but it continues the right search pattern: hold K fixed, change one architectural idea, and see whether encoder expressivity can move proxy FVU. If this also comes back flat, the current built-in families are not yet showing a path to the target.
notes_to_memory: residual_topk at K=128/ef8/signum archived under normal runtime with proxy FVU 0.3055884689092636, so the residual two-branch prototype did not improve on the anchor.
Round 4 opens factorized_topk as the next expressive family to test at fixed K=128 before considering lower-K or cost-driven moves.
next_hypotheses: If factorized_topk improves proxy FVU, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before promotion.
If factorized_topk is also flat or worse, shift from parameter-only family swapping to a code edit that introduces a genuinely new encoder family or materially changes one incubating family.

action.hypothesis: factorized_topk likely failed because its hidden mixer is too narrow and lossy; widening that stage and adding a gate should preserve more information before latent selection and materially improve FVU. Re-test factorized_topk at K=128 after this single architectural stabilization edit.
action.summary: The first factorized_topk prototype was much worse than the anchor, but its implementation used a plain hidden layer with width equal to d_in, which is a likely bottleneck rather than a decisive family verdict. This round keeps the same family and K while upgrading the encoder to a wider gated mixer.
action.self_review: This is a coherent follow-up on the only family that produced a strongly different signal, and it targets an obvious implementation bottleneck instead of abandoning the branch too early. The edit increases encoder compute, so even a quality gain still needs later cost scrutiny.
notes_to_memory: The original factorized_topk prototype likely suffered from an overly narrow d_in-sized hidden bottleneck rather than only a bad family prior.
Round 5 stabilizes factorized_topk by replacing the narrow ReLU mixer with a wider gated hidden stage before latent projection.
next_hypotheses: If the stabilized factorized_topk proxy improves substantially, consider promotion or a follow-up quality pass with optimizer tuning.
If the stabilized factorized_topk remains poor under normal runtime, deprioritize the current built-in families and move to a genuinely new encoder family rather than more local tuning.

mixture_topk.design_hypothesis: A soft mixture-of-experts encoder may beat the current K=128 topk anchor by giving the encoder more routing expressivity without the hard-capacity bottlenecks of grouped routing. Test the existing mixture_topk family at the same K=128 and expansion factor as a quality-first proxy comparison.
next_steps: If mixture_topk shows even modest proxy FVU gain, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before judging promotion.
If mixture_topk is flat or worse under normal runtime, try another expressive family such as residual_topk or factorized_topk at the same K=128 anchor.

residual_topk.design_hypothesis: A residual two-branch encoder may reduce FVU at K=128 by letting the second encoder pass target what the first branch misses, which is directly aligned with the reconstruction objective. Test residual_topk at the same K=128 anchor point as a quality-first architecture probe.
next_steps: If residual_topk shows proxy improvement, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before promotion.
If residual_topk is also flat or worse, try factorized_topk next rather than returning to smaller-K search.

factorized_topk.design_hypothesis: factorized_topk likely failed because its hidden mixer is too narrow and lossy; widening that stage and adding a gate should preserve more information before latent selection and materially improve FVU. Re-test factorized_topk at K=128 after this single architectural stabilization edit.
next_steps: If the stabilized factorized_topk proxy improves substantially, consider promotion or a follow-up quality pass with optimizer tuning.
If the stabilized factorized_topk remains poor under normal runtime, deprioritize the current built-in families and move to a genuinely new encoder family rather than more local tuning.

If the stabilized factorized_topk remains poor under normal runtime, deprioritize the current built-in families and move to a genuinely new encoder family rather than more local tuning.
